title: Ev Charger Network Expansion Acceleration
thesis: Rapid week-over-week growth in operational EV charging infrastructure signals infrastructure investment cycle acceleration and policy tailwinds, driving energy and industrial demand. EV charger buildout requires industrial equipment, electrical contractors, and grid upgrades; correlates with industrials capex cycles.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap public API for active charger counts by region, daily snapshots
signal_logic: If weekly charger count growth exceeds 1.2% and 20-day moving average of daily growth rate is positive, enter long
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly charger count growth exceeds 1.2% and 20-day moving average of daily growth rate is positive, enter long Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if weekly growth drops below 0.3% for 2 consecutive weeks
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap public API for active charger counts by region, daily snapshots via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Charger networks expand continuously; weekly growth crossing 1.2% threshold occurs 2–4 times per quarter in major markets.

data.source: OpenChargeMap public API for active charger counts by region, daily snapshots

schema.behavior: Rapid week-over-week growth in operational EV charging infrastructure signals infrastructure investment cycle acceleration and policy tailwinds, driving energy and industrial demand.

market_impact.rationale: EV charger buildout requires industrial equipment, electrical contractors, and grid upgrades; correlates with industrials capex cycles.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly charger count growth exceeds 1.2% and 20-day moving average of daily growth rate is positive, enter long
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if weekly growth drops below 0.3% for 2 consecutive weeks

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Charger networks expand continuously; weekly growth crossing 1.2% threshold occurs 2–4 times per quarter in major markets.

title: Earthquake Cluster Economic Disruption Trade
thesis: Clusters of moderate seismic activity in economically active regions trigger insurance and property damage expectations, boosting financials and industrials short-term positioning. Earthquake clusters drive insurance payouts, reinsurance demand, and property damage claims that benefit financial services and adjust valuations.
data_sources: USGS earthquake activity API, magnitude 4.5+ events within major US metros, daily updates
signal_logic: If 3+ magnitude 4.5+ earthquakes occur within 500km radius in 7 days, enter long financial sector
entry_exit: Entry: If 3+ magnitude 4.5+ earthquakes occur within 500km radius in 7 days, enter long financial sector Exit: Exit after 12 trading days or once seismic activity returns to baseline (no events in 5 days)
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use USGS earthquake activity API, magnitude 4.5+ events within major US metros, daily updates via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: US experiences 1–2 magnitude 4.5+ earthquake clusters per month; regional clustering within 7 days happens monthly on average.

data.source: USGS earthquake activity API, magnitude 4.5+ events within major US metros, daily updates

schema.behavior: Clusters of moderate seismic activity in economically active regions trigger insurance and property damage expectations, boosting financials and industrials short-term positioning.

market_impact.rationale: Earthquake clusters drive insurance payouts, reinsurance demand, and property damage claims that benefit financial services and adjust valuations.

trade_logic.entry: If 3+ magnitude 4.5+ earthquakes occur within 500km radius in 7 days, enter long financial sector
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 12 trading days or once seismic activity returns to baseline (no events in 5 days)

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: US experiences 1–2 magnitude 4.5+ earthquake clusters per month; regional clustering within 7 days happens monthly on average.

title: Freight Diesel Volatility Spike Contraction Signal
thesis: When diesel prices spike >5% week-over-week while container volumes contract >3% weekly, supply chain tightening creates logistics margin pressure and industrial demand signals. Diesel spikes + volume contractions signal demand shock and rising cost pressures on transport and manufacturing.
data_sources: FRED series GASDESW (diesel prices) and port_container_volume adapter (weekly TEU data), daily/weekly updates
signal_logic: If GASDESW spike >5% WoW and port TEU volumes fall >3% WoW simultaneously, enter short industrials
entry_exit: Entry: If GASDESW spike >5% WoW and port TEU volumes fall >3% WoW simultaneously, enter short industrials Exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if either metric normalizes (diesel falls back <2% or TEU growth turns positive)
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series GASDESW (diesel prices) and port_container_volume adapter (weekly TEU data), daily/weekly updates via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Diesel and port volume volatility cross thresholds 2–3 times per quarter; simultaneous moves occur monthly or near-monthly.

schema.idea: Freight Diesel Volatility Spike Contraction Signal

data.source: FRED series GASDESW (diesel prices) and port_container_volume adapter (weekly TEU data), daily/weekly updates

schema.behavior: When diesel prices spike >5% week-over-week while container volumes contract >3% weekly, supply chain tightening creates logistics margin pressure and industrial demand signals.

market_impact.rationale: Diesel spikes + volume contractions signal demand shock and rising cost pressures on transport and manufacturing.

trade_logic.entry: If GASDESW spike >5% WoW and port TEU volumes fall >3% WoW simultaneously, enter short industrials
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if either metric normalizes (diesel falls back <2% or TEU growth turns positive)

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Diesel and port volume volatility cross thresholds 2–3 times per quarter; simultaneous moves occur monthly or near-monthly.

title: Google Trends Job Search Panic Spike
thesis: Sharp spikes in job-search-related Google searches signal layoff fears or employment anxiety, causing consumer spending pullbacks and volatility in discretionary sectors. Job search anxiety correlates with consumer confidence collapse and discretionary spending cuts.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'how to write a resume' and 'interview tips,' normalized to baseline
signal_logic: If either 'resume' or 'interview tips' search volume jumps >40% week-over-week above 12-week moving average, short discretionary
entry_exit: Entry: If either 'resume' or 'interview tips' search volume jumps >40% week-over-week above 12-week moving average, short discretionary Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if search interest returns to <110% of 12-week MA
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for 'how to write a resume' and 'interview tips,' normalized to baseline via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Job-search Google Trends spike above 40% threshold 3–5 times per quarter due to weekly earnings seasons, layoff news cycles, and seasonal hiring patterns.

schema.idea: Google Trends Job Search Panic Spike

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'how to write a resume' and 'interview tips,' normalized to baseline

schema.behavior: Sharp spikes in job-search-related Google searches signal layoff fears or employment anxiety, causing consumer spending pullbacks and volatility in discretionary sectors.

market_impact.rationale: Job search anxiety correlates with consumer confidence collapse and discretionary spending cuts.

trade_logic.entry: If either 'resume' or 'interview tips' search volume jumps >40% week-over-week above 12-week moving average, short discretionary
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if search interest returns to <110% of 12-week MA

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Job-search Google Trends spike above 40% threshold 3–5 times per quarter due to weekly earnings seasons, layoff news cycles, and seasonal hiring patterns.

title: Weather Extreme Utility Demand Whipsaw
thesis: Extreme temperature swings (>15°F deviation from seasonal normal across multiple regions) drive sudden utility demand spikes and grid stress, boosting energy and utilities sector volatility. Weather extremes force emergency grid operations and spot market pricing spikes, benefiting utilities operators.
data_sources: Open-Meteo daily temperature extremes (max/min) for top 20 US metros, aggregated deviation from 30-year normal
signal_logic: If 8+ metros show temperature deviation >15°F from 30-year normal on same day, enter long utilities
entry_exit: Entry: If 8+ metros show temperature deviation >15°F from 30-year normal on same day, enter long utilities Exit: Exit after 6 trading days or if temperature volatility normalizes (deviations <8°F for 3 consecutive days)
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Open-Meteo daily temperature extremes (max/min) for top 20 US metros, aggregated deviation from 30-year normal via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Synchronized multi-region temperature extremes occur 8–12 times per year; seasonal transitions (spring/fall) generate daily crossings.

data.source: Open-Meteo daily temperature extremes (max/min) for top 20 US metros, aggregated deviation from 30-year normal

schema.behavior: Extreme temperature swings (>15°F deviation from seasonal normal across multiple regions) drive sudden utility demand spikes and grid stress, boosting energy and utilities sector volatility.

market_impact.rationale: Weather extremes force emergency grid operations and spot market pricing spikes, benefiting utilities operators.

trade_logic.entry: If 8+ metros show temperature deviation >15°F from 30-year normal on same day, enter long utilities
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 6 trading days or if temperature volatility normalizes (deviations <8°F for 3 consecutive days)

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Synchronized multi-region temperature extremes occur 8–12 times per year; seasonal transitions (spring/fall) generate daily crossings.

title: Healthcare Cost Search Anxiety Surge
thesis: Spikes in healthcare cost searches signal consumer stress about medical expenses, often preceding healthcare sector volatility and consumer spending pullbacks. Healthcare cost anxiety may depress elective procedures and consumer pharmaceutical adherence, pressuring healthcare revenues.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'prescription drug prices' and 'medical bill debt,' normalized to 12-week baseline
signal_logic: If either search term jumps >50% WoW above 12-week MA, short healthcare
entry_exit: Entry: If either search term jumps >50% WoW above 12-week MA, short healthcare Exit: Exit after 9 trading days or if search interest normalizes to <115% of 12-week MA
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for 'prescription drug prices' and 'medical bill debt,' normalized to 12-week baseline via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Healthcare cost search spikes >50% occur 2–4 times per quarter, especially around earnings seasons, policy announcements, and seasonal insurance renewal cycles.

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'prescription drug prices' and 'medical bill debt,' normalized to 12-week baseline

schema.behavior: Spikes in healthcare cost searches signal consumer stress about medical expenses, often preceding healthcare sector volatility and consumer spending pullbacks.

market_impact.rationale: Healthcare cost anxiety may depress elective procedures and consumer pharmaceutical adherence, pressuring healthcare revenues.

trade_logic.entry: If either search term jumps >50% WoW above 12-week MA, short healthcare
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 9 trading days or if search interest normalizes to <115% of 12-week MA

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Healthcare cost search spikes >50% occur 2–4 times per quarter, especially around earnings seasons, policy announcements, and seasonal insurance renewal cycles.

title: Attention Spike Supply Chain Bottleneck News Volume
thesis: Daily spikes in supply chain disruption news coverage correlate with industrial demand shocks and equity rotation into defensive sectors. Supply chain news spikes signal production headwinds and cost pressures that depress industrial equipment and manufacturing valuations.
data_sources: RSS feed article count monitoring 'supply chain disruption' and 'logistics backlog' across major financial news sites, daily updates
signal_logic: If daily RSS article count for supply chain keywords exceeds 150% of 20-day moving average, short industrials
entry_exit: Entry: If daily RSS article count for supply chain keywords exceeds 150% of 20-day moving average, short industrials Exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if RSS count returns to <120% of 20-day MA for 2 consecutive days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS feed article count monitoring 'supply chain disruption' and 'logistics backlog' across major financial news sites, daily updates via scrape (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Supply chain news spikes above 150% threshold occur 3–6 times per quarter due to port congestion, carrier bankruptcies, and seasonal demand shocks.

schema.idea: Attention Spike: Supply Chain Bottleneck News Volume

data.source: RSS feed article count monitoring 'supply chain disruption' and 'logistics backlog' across major financial news sites, daily updates

schema.behavior: Daily spikes in supply chain disruption news coverage correlate with industrial demand shocks and equity rotation into defensive sectors.

market_impact.rationale: Supply chain news spikes signal production headwinds and cost pressures that depress industrial equipment and manufacturing valuations.

trade_logic.entry: If daily RSS article count for supply chain keywords exceeds 150% of 20-day moving average, short industrials
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if RSS count returns to <120% of 20-day MA for 2 consecutive days

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Supply chain news spikes above 150% threshold occur 3–6 times per quarter due to port congestion, carrier bankruptcies, and seasonal demand shocks.

title: Small Cap Volatility Compression Breakout
thesis: When IWM 30-day realized volatility falls below 12% while IWM lags SPY by >2% cumulatively over 15 days, subsequent mean reversion often triggers sharp small-cap rallies. Small-cap outperformance correlates with risk-on rotation and consumer discretionary strength.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily price data for IWM (Russell 2000) and SPY, 30-day realized volatility, price_only adapter
signal_logic: If IWM 30-day realized vol <12% AND IWM underperforms SPY by >2% over prior 15 days AND IWM closes above 5-day MA, enter long
entry_exit: Entry: If IWM 30-day realized vol <12% AND IWM underperforms SPY by >2% over prior 15 days AND IWM closes above 5-day MA, enter long Exit: Exit after 8 trading days or once IWM outperforms SPY by >1.5% over 3 days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily price data for IWM (Russell 2000) and SPY, 30-day realized volatility, price_only adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Small-cap volatility compression + underperformance occurs 3–4 times per quarter, especially after Fed pauses or risk sentiment shifts.

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily price data for IWM (Russell 2000) and SPY, 30-day realized volatility, price_only adapter

schema.behavior: When IWM 30-day realized volatility falls below 12% while IWM lags SPY by >2% cumulatively over 15 days, subsequent mean reversion often triggers sharp small-cap rallies.

market_impact.rationale: Small-cap outperformance correlates with risk-on rotation and consumer discretionary strength.

trade_logic.entry: If IWM 30-day realized vol <12% AND IWM underperforms SPY by >2% over prior 15 days AND IWM closes above 5-day MA, enter long
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 8 trading days or once IWM outperforms SPY by >1.5% over 3 days

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Small-cap volatility compression + underperformance occurs 3–4 times per quarter, especially after Fed pauses or risk sentiment shifts.

title: Commercial Real Estate Financing Stress Google Trends
thesis: Spikes in commercial real estate financing search terms signal financial distress in office/retail property markets, pressuring REIT and financial valuations. CRE financing stress directly impacts REIT returns and commercial property values.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'commercial mortgage rates' and 'CMBS default,' normalized to 12-week baseline
signal_logic: If either search term spikes >45% WoW above 12-week MA, short real estate
entry_exit: Entry: If either search term spikes >45% WoW above 12-week MA, short real estate Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if search interest normalizes to <120% of 12-week MA
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for 'commercial mortgage rates' and 'CMBS default,' normalized to 12-week baseline via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Commercial mortgage and CMBS stress searches spike >45% WoW 2–3 times per quarter, driven by rate cycle turns and quarterly earnings disappointments.

data.source: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'commercial mortgage rates' and 'CMBS default,' normalized to 12-week baseline

market_impact.rationale: CRE financing stress directly impacts REIT returns and commercial property values.

trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if search interest normalizes to <120% of 12-week MA

title: Energy Sector Relative Strength Momentum Fade
thesis: When XLE outperforms SPY by >4% over 10 days but XLE daily close falls below its 5-day moving average, mean reversion pullback often follows within days. Energy momentum exhaustion signals profit-taking and demand concerns in commodity-linked sectors.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLE and SPY, relative strength index (XLE vs SPY 20-day momentum), price_only adapter
signal_logic: If XLE leads SPY >4% over 10 days AND XLE closes below 5-day MA AND MACD(12,26) turns negative, short energy
entry_exit: Entry: If XLE leads SPY >4% over 10 days AND XLE closes below 5-day MA AND MACD(12,26) turns negative, short energy Exit: Exit after 6 trading days or if XLE closes above 10-day MA
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLE and SPY, relative strength index (XLE vs SPY 20-day momentum), price_only adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Energy outperformance spikes >4% over 10-day windows occur 3–5 times per quarter; momentum exhaustion patterns fire weekly.

schema.idea: Energy Sector Relative Strength Momentum Fade

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLE and SPY, relative strength index (XLE vs SPY 20-day momentum), price_only adapter

schema.behavior: When XLE outperforms SPY by >4% over 10 days but XLE daily close falls below its 5-day moving average, mean reversion pullback often follows within days.

market_impact.rationale: Energy momentum exhaustion signals profit-taking and demand concerns in commodity-linked sectors.

trade_logic.entry: If XLE leads SPY >4% over 10 days AND XLE closes below 5-day MA AND MACD(12,26) turns negative, short energy
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 6 trading days or if XLE closes above 10-day MA

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Energy outperformance spikes >4% over 10-day windows occur 3–5 times per quarter; momentum exhaustion patterns fire weekly.
